page: Amazon Web Services t1.micro is a first-generation burstable performance micro instance. It is built on the Xen hypervisor and features a legacy 32-bit i386 CPU architecture. The instance is equipped with a single dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2670 v2 core operating at 2.5 GHz. This processor includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.25 MB of L2 cache, and 25 MB of L3 cache. Memory capacity is highly constrained at 0.6 GB of RAM, which equates to approximately 0.61 GB per core. There is no local storage or GPU acceleration bundled with this instance type, and the baseline network bandwidth is rated at 0 Gbps.

Benchmark data highlights the performance limitations of this hardware profile. While the stress-ng single-core CPU performance achieves an average score of 1856.98, placing it in the middle 50% tier, its multi-core performance falls into the bottom 10% with an identical score due to the single-core limitation. Other metrics, such as the Passmark CPU score of 1121.29 and the Passmark memory score of 0.0, sit in the bottom 10%. Cryptographic and vector instruction sets are absent, as indicated by the lack of AVX, AVX512, and AES support, which is reflected in a poor Passmark CPU encryption score of 236.21. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is measured at 105.19 GB/sec, and database operations score 0.0, both representing bottom-tier performance.

The primary tradeoff of the t1.micro is its extremely limited resource profile, particularly the 0.6 GB memory capacity and lack of modern instruction sets like AVX or AES. This makes it unsuitable for modern memory-intensive applications, database hosting, or cryptographic workloads. However, it serves as a highly cost-efficient entry point for basic administrative tasks, lightweight scripting, or simple static web serving, where throughput requirements are minimal. Redis SET operations reach 0.37 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput is limited to 1.62 GB/sec, confirming its suitability only for low-traffic, non-critical utility workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services t1.micro is a first-generation burstable micro instance designed for low-resource utility workloads. Running on the Xen hypervisor, it features a single-core Intel Xeon E5-2670 v2 processor with a 32-bit i386 architecture, operating at 2.5 GHz. The instance is configured with 0.6 GB of RAM, no local storage, and a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps. Benchmark results place the t1.micro in the bottom 10% for most performance categories, including a Passmark CPU score of 1121.29 and a memory score of 0.0. It lacks modern CPU instruction sets such as AVX and AES, which limits its cryptographic and vector processing capabilities. Given these resource constraints, the t1.micro is best suited for lightweight administrative tasks, basic scripting, or low-traffic static web serving rather than database hosting or intensive computational workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services t1.micro is a first-generation burstable micro instance featuring a 1-core Intel Xeon E5-2670 v2 CPU, 0.6 GB of RAM, and a 32-bit i386 architecture on the Xen hypervisor.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services t1.micro is a 1-core, 32-bit burstable instance with 0.6 GB RAM, designed for lightweight utility workloads on the Xen hypervisor.
tagline: A highly constrained, single-core legacy micro instance designed for basic utility scripting and low-traffic administrative tasks.
bullet_points: Single-core Intel Xeon E5-2670 v2 processor running at 2.5 GHz
Extremely limited memory capacity of 0.6 GB RAM
Legacy 32-bit i386 architecture running on the Xen hypervisor
No local storage and 0 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Best suited for low-traffic static web serving and basic scripting
